top_categories.workout_context: 10,400ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before July.
64 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 64 miles.
Base fitness for Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
162ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,400ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
At Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre’s 64 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Granfondo Sestriere Colle delle Finestre with 10,400ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 64 miles of mixed terrain in Cesana Torinese, Piedmont, Italy.
